author: Marcus Chen
title: OpenAI's Realtime API Is Reshaping Customer Conversations
description: Sub-300ms latency voice AI changes what's possible in customer-facing applications. Here's what marketing teams can build with it — and what they shouldn't.

sections.p: OpenAI's Realtime API delivers voice AI responses in under 300 milliseconds — fast enough that the pause isn't noticeable in a natural conversation. That latency breakthrough unlocks a class of customer-facing applications that previous voice AI couldn't support: real sales conversations, dynamic product discovery, and live support interactions that don't feel like talking to a phone tree.

list: Inbound qualification: an AI that conducts a real 5-minute discovery call, qualifies against ICP criteria, and books a meeting with a human rep — without the prospect knowing the difference.
Product demos: a voice guide that adapts to the prospect's questions in real time, surfacing the relevant features without a scripted flow.
Re-engagement calls: outbound AI calls to lapsed customers with personalized context from the CRM, far more effective than automated email re-engagement.
Customer success check-ins: proactive calls to customer cohorts showing churn risk signals, gathering feedback and flagging issues before they escalate.
Event follow-up: calls to event attendees within 24 hours while the interaction is still fresh.

sections.p: The FTC and several state AGs have published guidance that AI-powered voice calls must disclose their AI nature at the start of the interaction. Beyond compliance, disclosure is also good strategy — it sets the right expectation, reduces frustration when the AI can't handle edge cases, and earns the trust that makes the interaction more productive.

The disclosure doesn't have to be heavy: 'I'm an AI assistant at NetWebMedia — I can help you with X and Y. If you'd prefer to speak with a human, just say so and I'll connect you now.' That framing sets expectations without undermining the experience.

The Realtime API in isolation is a voice AI. Connected to your CRM, calendar, and product data, it becomes a customer interaction system. The quality of the customer experience is directly proportional to the quality of the data the AI has access to in real time. A call agent that can see the prospect's company, their usage history, and the notes from their last interaction produces a fundamentally different experience than one that starts blank.

Complex negotiations, sensitive customer situations, and interactions where the human relationship is the product don't belong in voice AI. The best implementations are deliberate about where AI handles the interaction end-to-end and where it hands off — and that hand-off needs to be seamless, with full context transfer.

sections.quote: The question isn't whether AI voice can pass as human. It's whether it delivers a better outcome for the customer than a human could at the same scale. In high-volume, well-defined interactions, it does.
